paper_title: Study of Aeolian Dust Accumulation in Archaeological Structures

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from archaeological structures and surrounding areas to investigate Holocene dust accumulation.

steps.description: Investigate sediments potentially comprising Holocene dust from archaeological structures in the northern Negev and Petra area.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from archaeological structures

steps.description: Set up three dry marble dust collectors near archaeological sites and continuously sample them.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and sample dry marble dust collectors

steps.description: Sample natural soils and geological outcrops surrounding the site up to 200-m distance as references.
steps.searchable_summary: collect reference soil and geological samples

methods.description: Analysis of collected sediment samples to determine their physical and chemical properties.

steps.description: Determine color, bulk density, pH, organic matter, electrical conductivity, contents of calcium carbonate, and chemical composition of the studied sediments according to standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze physical and chemical properties of sediments

steps.description: Analyze grain sizes by wet sieving for sand fractions, and using Malvern MasterSizer 3000 laser diffraction analyzer and Sedigraph grain size analyzer for silt and clay fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform grain size analysis on sediment samples

methods.description: Statistical procedures applied to analyze grain size distributions and model types of deposits.

steps.description: Apply Gradistat for Excel and EMMAgeo v0.94 R package for grain size distributions including end-member analysis and principle component analysis (PCA) using the R-function prcomp.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA and end-member analysis on grain size data

steps.description: Statistically model types of deposits using the R package randomForest.
